2606.31167.title: MIRTH: Mutual-Information Reasoning with Temporal Hubs for Vision-Language-Action Agents
2606.31167.paper_summary: VLA models have emerged as a powerful paradigm for transferring semantic knowledge from web-scale data to physical robotic control. However, current single-frame architectures suffer from intrinsic limitations: temporal myopia that discards historical dynamics, reasoning gaps between high-level instructions and low-level motor commands, and inference inefficiency due to autoregressive scalar decoding. In this work, we propose MIRTH, a unified framework designed to address these challenges. MIRTH augments a pretrained VLA backbone with three key innovations: (1) dual-scale temporal memory hubs that compress long-term scene evolution and short-term motion trends into compact embeddings; (2) latent reasoning tokens optimized via a mutual-information objective carving out a semantic plan space to align multimodal context with action trajectories; and (3) a parallel action decoding scheme that replaces autoregressive generation with vector-wise prediction to maximize control throughput. Extensive evaluations on the LIBERO simulation benchmark and a real-world LeRobot platform demonstrate that MIRTH achieves state-of-the-art performance and exhibiting emergent error recovery capabilities. The codes and collected datasets are released at http://github.com/kiva12138/mirth.
2606.31167.paper_summary_zh: VLA模型已成為將語義知識從網絡規模數據轉移到物理機器人控制的強大範式。<lb/>然而，目前的單幀架構存在內在的限制：時間近視，忽略了歷史動態，高層指令與低層運動命令之間的推理差距，以及由於自回歸標量解碼造成的推理效率低下。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們提出了MIRTH，一個旨在解決這些挑戰的統一框架。<lb/>MIRTH通過三個關鍵創新來增強預訓練的VLA骨幹：(1) 雙尺度時間記憶樞紐，將長期場景演變和短期運動趨勢壓縮為緊湊的嵌入；(2) 通過互信息目標優化的潛在推理標記，劃分出語義計劃空間，以將多模態上下文與行動軌跡對齊；以及(3) 一種並行行動解碼方案，將自回歸生成替換為向量預測，以最大化控制吞吐量。<lb/>在LIBERO模擬基準和現實世界的LeRobot平台上進行的廣泛評估表明，MIRTH實現了最先進的性能，並展現了新興的錯誤恢復能力。<lb/>代碼和收集的數據集已在http://github.com/kiva12138/mirth上發布。
2606.31167.author: Hao Sun et.al.
2606.31167.authors: Hao Sun, Yu Song, Shiyu Teng, Ziwei Niu, Yen-Wei Chen
